dict.string: TransferQueen needs access to your Apple Music and media library to allow you to select and securely transfer audio files to nearby devices.

TransferQueen uses your camera to scan QR codes to connect with nearby devices and to capture photos or videos you want to share. For example, you can scan a QR code displayed on another device to instantly establish a peer-to-peer connection for file sharing.

TransferQueen requires access to your contacts to allow you to select and share contact details with nearby devices over a direct peer-to-peer connection. For example, you can send a business card or contact info to a friend's phone instantly.

TransferQueen accesses your Documents folder to let you select files to share with nearby devices and to save files received from others. For example, you can pick a PDF from your Documents folder and send it wirelessly to a nearby contact.

TransferQueen uses your local network to discover and connect to nearby devices for direct peer-to-peer file sharing without the internet. For example, it finds other phones on the same Wi-Fi network so you can instantly send photos, videos, or documents to them.
TransferQueen uses location services to help discover nearby devices on the same network for fast file sharing.
TransferQueen requires microphone access for video recording and camera functionality.

TransferQueen saves photos and videos received from nearby devices directly to your photo library. For example, when a contact sends you an image over a peer-to-peer connection, it will be saved to your Photos app.

TransferQueen accesses your photo library so you can select photos and videos to share wirelessly with nearby devices. For example, you can pick multiple photos and send them to a friend's device without using mobile data or the internet.
